rulings.Case Title: THOMAS MCALLISTER et al VS. ALLIED PACKING & SUPPLY, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 26, 2015 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Sears, Roebuck and Co.'s motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication is denied as untimely. Defendant failed to provide proper notice pursuant to C.C.P. sections 437c(a), 1010.6, 12a and 12c. Plaintiffs' objection based on timeliness is sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS MCALLISTER et al VS. ALLIED PACKING & SUPPLY, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 26, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant BorgWarner Morse Tec Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. McAllister was exposed to a BorgWarner product. Defendant's objection to Mr. McAllister's deposition testimony identifying BorgWarner clutches is overruled. Mr. McAllister's deposition testimony is admissible. (In re Cindy L. (1997) 17 Cal.4th 15.) Mr. McAllister's deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to sustain its initial burden of negating an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. First, when read in conjunction with Mr. McAllister's deposition testimony, the declarations of Carole Cameron, Bryon Frantz and Ted Loroña merely create a triable issue whether the clutches purchased from Linda Vista Auto/Cameron's were BorgWarner. Second, those declarations are insufficient to create an impossibility that the clutches purchased from Linda Vista Auto/Cameron's were BorgWarner. Plaintiffs' objections to the declaration of Carole Cameron in its entirety are overruled and as to paragraph 3 stating that Cameron's was a NAPA store and only carried NAPA products plaintiffs' objections are sustained as to all times outside the 1952-1956 range. Plaintiffs' objections to the declarations of Bryon Frantz and Ted Loroña are overruled. The declarations meet the requirements of CRC 3.1115. Defendant's alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is off calendar because plaintiffs have dismissed such claim. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
